content.problemStatement:
You are given 222 integers NNN and MMM. You also have an empty set SSS.

Consider an array AAA of length NNN such that 1≤Ai≤M1 \leq A_i \leq M1≤Ai​≤M for each 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N (there are MNM^NMN such arrays in total).

For each of these MNM^NMN arrays, insert **all** of its longest non-decreasing subsequences into SSS.

Output the size of SSS. Since the size of SSS might be large, output it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

Note that since SSS is a set, it stores only distinct values. For example, when N=3N = 3N=3 and M=2M = 2M=2, the sequence [1,1][1, 1][1,1] is a longest non-decreasing subsequence for both A=[1,2,1]A = [1, 2, 1]A=[1,2,1] and for A=[2,1,1]A = [2, 1, 1]A=[2,1,1] — however, it will only be present once in SSS.

Please look at the sample cases below for an explained example.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the size of set SSS, modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There is only one possible array [1,1,1][1, 1, 1][1,1,1], whose longest non-decreasing subsequence is also unique, namely [1,1,1][1, 1, 1][1,1,1]. So, the size of set SSS is 111.
Test case 222: There are 32=93^2 = 932=9 possible arrays. These arrays and their corresponding sequences are as follows:

[1,1][1, 1][1,1] contributes [1,1][1, 1][1,1] to SSS
[1,2][1, 2][1,2] contributes [1,2][1, 2][1,2] to SSS
[1,3][1, 3][1,3] contributes [1,3][1, 3][1,3] to SSS
[2,1][2, 1][2,1] contributes [2][2][2] and [1][1][1] to SSS
[2,2][2, 2][2,2] contributes [2,2][2, 2][2,2] to SSS
[2,3][2, 3][2,3] contributes [2,3][2, 3][2,3] to SSS
[3,1][3, 1][3,1] contributes [3][3][3] and [1][1][1] to SSS
[3,2][3, 2][3,2] contributes [3][3][3] and [2][2][2] to SSS
[3,3][3, 3][3,3] contributes [3,3][3, 3][3,3] to SSS

Finally, it can be seen that S={[1],[2],[3],[1,1],[2,2],[3,3],[1,2],[1,3],[2,3]}S = \{[1], [2], [3], [1, 1], [2, 2], [3, 3], [1, 2], [1, 3], [2, 3] \}S={[1],[2],[3],[1,1],[2,2],[3,3],[1,2],[1,3],[2,3]} has a size of 999.
Test case 333: Set SSS contains 555 elements, one corresponding to each of the 555 possible arrays.
